description: Microsserviço de matches do FindMyParty — consome evento profile.profile.created via RabbitMQ, persiste perfis localmente e expõe endpoints HTTP para criar e listar matches entre perfis.

userStories.title: Setup inicial do projeto
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the base directory structure and config files for MatchService so that it follows the project's hexagonal architecture pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Estrutura de pastas criada: src/domain/, src/application/, src/adapters/inbound/, src/adapters/outbound/, src/config/, src/shared/, src/migrations/
package.json com name: match-service, type: module, scripts start/dev/test/migrate

Dependências instaladas: fastify, @fastify/cors, @fastify/helmet, @fastify/swagger, @fastify/swagger-ui, kysely, pg, amqplib, zod, pino, pino-loki, @opentelemetry/sdk-node, @opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node, @opentelemetry/exporter-metrics-otlp-http, @opentelemetry/exporter-trace-otlp-http, @sentry/node; devDependency: vitest
.env.example com PORT, DATABASE_URL (localhost:5434), RABBITMQ_URL, JWT_SECRET, NODE_ENV, LOG_LEVEL, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, SENTRY_DSN
Dockerfile funcional baseado no skeleton-service adaptado para match-service
.gitignore cobrindo node_modules, .env, dist

userStories.title: Configuração e validação de ambiente
userStories.description: As a developer, I want environment variables validated with zod at boot so that missing config is caught immediately with a clear message.
acceptanceCriteria: src/config/env.js valida PORT, DATABASE_URL, RABBITMQ_URL, JWT_SECRET, NODE_ENV, LOG_LEVEL, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT com zod
SENTRY_DSN é opcional (zod .optional())
Processo encerra com mensagem clara se variável obrigatória estiver ausente
process.env só é acessado dentro de src/config/env.js
DATABASE_URL no .env.example aponta para postgresql://user:password@localhost:5434/match

userStories.title: Setup de migrations e migration da tabela profile
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the migration system configured and the profile table created so that profiles received via RabbitMQ events can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: src/adapters/outbound/db/migrator.js configura FileMigrationProvider do kysely apontando para src/migrations/
Script migrate no package.json executa: node --env-file=.env src/adapters/outbound/db/migrator.js
Migration 001_create_profile.js em src/migrations/ cria tabela profile com id_profile (VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY) e name (VARCHAR NOT NULL)
Migration usa IF NOT EXISTS (idempotente)
npm run migrate executa sem erros com banco disponível

userStories.title: Migration da tabela match_tb
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the match_tb table created via migration so that matches between profiles can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration 002_create_match_tb.js em src/migrations/ cria tabela match_tb com id_profile1 (VARCHAR NOT NULL), id_profile2 (VARCHAR NOT NULL), data (TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW())
Chave primária composta (id_profile1, id_profile2) definida via sql raw: PRIMARY KEY (id_profile1, id_profile2)
id_profile1 e id_profile2 são foreign keys para profile.id_profile
Migration usa IF NOT EXISTS (idempotente)
npm run migrate executa ambas as migrations sem erros

userStories.title: Shared: logger e errors
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared logger and error classes available so that all layers of the application can use them consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: src/shared/logger.js exporta instância pino configurada com level via env.LOG_LEVEL
src/shared/errors.js exporta: AppError(message, statusCode, code), NotFoundError(resource), ValidationError(message), UnauthorizedError(), ConflictError(message)
Nenhum import de adapters/ ou config/ dentro de shared/

userStories.title: Entidades do domínio (Profile e Match)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Profile and Match domain entities so that business data is modeled without any infrastructure dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/entities/profile.js exporta createProfile({ id_profile, name }) que valida ambos obrigatórios e retorna objeto { id_profile, name }

src/domain/entities/match.js exporta createMatch({ id_profile1, id_profile2, data }) que valida id_profile1 e id_profile2 obrigatórios, data default para new Date(), e retorna objeto { id_profile1, id_profile2, data }
Domínio não importa nada de adapters/, config/ ou módulos Node.js built-in

userStories.title: Ports inbound e outbound
userStories.description: As a developer, I want well-defined port interfaces so that the separation between domain and infrastructure is clear.
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/ports/outbound/profile-repository.js documenta via JSDoc: save(profile), findById(id_profile)
src/domain/ports/outbound/match-repository.js documenta via JSDoc: save(match), findAll()
src/domain/ports/outbound/event-publisher.js documenta via JSDoc: publish(routingKey, payload)
src/domain/ports/inbound/match-service.js documenta via JSDoc: createMatch(data), listMatches()

userStories.title: Use cases (CreateProfile, CreateMatch, ListMatches)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want use cases implementing business logic that depend only on port interfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/use-cases/create-profile.js: recebe { id_profile, name }, cria entidade Profile via createProfile(), salva via IProfileRepository, loga a operação

src/domain/use-cases/create-match.js: recebe { id_profile1, id_profile2 }, valida que ids são diferentes (lança ValidationError se iguais), cria Match com data = new Date(), salva via IMatchRepository, publica evento match.match.created via IEventPublisher
src/domain/use-cases/list-matches.js: retorna todos os matches via IMatchRepository.findAll()
Nenhum use case importa de adapters/ ou config/

userStories.title: Application service (MatchApplicationService)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an application service orchestrating use cases with dependency injection so that infrastructure adapters are wired at the composition root.
acceptanceCriteria: src/application/services/match-application-service.js exporta classe MatchApplicationService
Constructor recebe (profileRepository, matchRepository, eventPublisher)
Método createMatch(data) instancia e executa CreateMatchUseCase
Método listMatches() instancia e executa ListMatchesUseCase
Implementa o contrato IMatchService

userStories.title: Adapter outbound — DB client e repositórios PostgreSQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I want kysely repositories implementing the outbound ports so that domain data is persisted in PostgreSQL.
acceptanceCriteria: src/adapters/outbound/db/client.js exporta singleton kysely conectado via env.DATABASE_URL com interface Database tipada via JSDoc (tabelas: profile, match_tb)
src/adapters/outbound/db/profile-repository.js implementa save(profile) e findById(id_profile) usando kysely na tabela profile
src/adapters/outbound/db/match-repository.js implementa save(match) e findAll() usando kysely na tabela match_tb
Nenhuma lógica de negócio dentro dos repositórios

userStories.title: Adapter outbound — RabbitMQ event publisher
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RabbitMQ publisher implementing IEventPublisher so that domain events are sent to the message broker.
acceptanceCriteria: src/adapters/outbound/messaging/publisher.js exporta classe RabbitMQEventPublisher
Método connect(channel) armazena o canal amqplib
Método publish(routingKey, payload) serializa payload para JSON e publica no exchange match (durable: true, type: topic) com a routing key fornecida
Erros de publish são logados sem derrubar o processo

userStories.title: Adapter inbound — Subscriber RabbitMQ para profile.profile.created
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RabbitMQ subscriber that consumes profile.profile.created and creates the profile locally.
acceptanceCriteria: src/adapters/inbound/messaging/subscriber.js exporta registerSubscribers(channel, createProfileUseCase)
Declara exchange profile como topic durable, queue match-service.profile.profile.created com dead letter exchange configurado para DLQ match-service.profile.profile.created.dlq
Binding key profile.profile.created
Ao receber mensagem: parseia JSON, extrai { id_profile, name }, executa createProfileUseCase
Sucesso: channel.ack(msg); erro de parse ou execução: loga o erro e channel.nack(msg, false, false)

userStories.title: Adapter inbound — HTTP server e auth middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Fastify server configured with cors, helmet, error handler and auth middleware so that routes are protected and errors are formatted consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: src/adapters/inbound/http/server.js configura Fastify com @fastify/cors, @fastify/helmet e error handler global que retorna { error: { code, message } }

src/adapters/inbound/http/middleware/auth.js verifica presença e formato Bearer <token> no header Authorization; retorna 401 (UnauthorizedError) se ausente ou inválido; injeta req.user com payload placeholder quando válido

userStories.title: Adapter inbound — HTTP routes de Match e health check
userStories.description: As an API client, I want HTTP endpoints to create and list matches, and a health check endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: src/adapters/inbound/http/routes/match.routes.js registra POST /matches com body zod { id_profile1: string, id_profile2: string }; retorna { data: { id_profile1, id_profile2, data } } status 201; requer auth middleware
GET /matches retorna { data: [...] } status 200; requer auth middleware
src/adapters/inbound/http/routes/health.routes.js registra GET /health sem autenticação; retorna { status: 'ok', dependencies: { postgres: 'ok', rabbitmq: 'ok', otel: 'ok' } } status 200
Erros seguem formato { error: { code, message } }

userStories.title: Observabilidade — telemetry, logger e métricas
userStories.description: As an operator, I want full OpenTelemetry and Sentry setup so that the service is observable from day one.
acceptanceCriteria: src/shared/telemetry.js exporta função initTelemetry() que inicializa OTel SDK com serviceName match-service e exporta traces via OTLP HTTP para OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
GET /metrics exposto para Prometheus via @opentelemetry/exporter-metrics-otlp-http ou endpoint nativo do SDK
Sentry inicializado em main.js se SENTRY_DSN estiver presente
Nenhum dado sensível nos logs ou spans

userStories.title: Boot sequence — main.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I want main.js to follow the boot sequence defined in CLAUDE.md so that all infrastructure is ready before the service accepts traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: src/main.js executa na ordem: (1) initTelemetry(), (2) validar env, (3) conectar DB via client.js, (4) executar migrateToLatest(), (5) conectar RabbitMQ via amqplib, (6) instanciar publisher e chamar connect(channel), (7) registrar subscribers via registerSubscribers(), (8) instanciar repositórios e application service, (9) iniciar HTTP server
Graceful shutdown em SIGTERM e SIGINT: fecha server HTTP, fecha conexão RabbitMQ, fecha DB
Logs pino claros em cada etapa do boot (ex: 'Database connected', 'Migrations applied', 'RabbitMQ connected', 'HTTP server listening on port X')

userStories.title: Docker-compose e start.sh adaptados
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the infrastructure files adapted from skeleton-service so that I can start the full stack with a single command.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml sobe Postgres 16-alpine com POSTGRES_DB: match, porta 5434:5432, volume match_postgres_data
docker-compose.yml sobe RabbitMQ 3.13-management-alpine na porta 5672:5672 e 15672:15672, volume match_rabbitmq_data
start.sh adaptado do skeleton-service: todas as referências skeleton → match, skeleton-service → match-service, porta pg_isready usa banco match
start.sh executa npm run migrate antes de npm start
start.sh tem permissão de execução (chmod +x start.sh no script de CI ou instrução no PRD)
